name: distribution-engine
description: Earned backlinks the white-hat way (OpenGrowth SPEC section 6): from ONE already-published article, draft platform-native syndication (Quora/Reddit answer, Medium/Substack repost with rel=canonical, LinkedIn, HARO), honesty-review it, then hold in a human-approval column — a person posts each piece in context. No link exchange, no auto-posting, no PHI.

steps.title: Draft earned-link syndication: {topic}
steps.description: From the already-published {project} article at {article_url} on "{topic}", draft platform-native copies that each earn their place on their own merit: a genuinely useful Quora or Reddit answer that cites the article, a Medium or Substack repost carrying a rel=canonical tag back to {article_url}, a LinkedIn article, and a HARO/journalist pitch. Make each one contextual and distinct — never one blurb mass-pasted. Cite only what the article actually says. This is a DRAFT package for a human to post; you never post it yourself.

steps.title: Honesty + white-hat review: {topic}
steps.description: Review every syndication draft against the Shaula honesty rules AND the white-hat bar: each must add real value in its own context, carry a rel=canonical tag wherever it republishes the article, and contain no fabricated claim. Flag anything that reads as a mass-paste or a link-exchange pattern. Hold the package until it is clean.

steps.title: Human gate — a person posts the earned links: {topic}
steps.description: HUMAN GATE: a person reviews the clean syndication package and posts each piece themselves, in context, on the platform. The agent never auto-posts — that is the line between an earned link and a spam pattern. After a human posts, record where the article was placed so the practice can see its real, earned backlinks.
